old_claim.note: Aseptic Surgery provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Laparoscopic operations are surgical procedures performed under sterile operating-room practice; asepsis is clinical context rather than hospital IT.

source_shape.source_locator: JSLS history article describing laparoscopic cholecystectomy as an operative surgical procedure with specialized instruments.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The article discusses laparoscopic cholecystectomy as a surgical operation and traces the instruments and courses that supported clinical adoption.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports aseptic surgery as clinical context because the node is an operative procedure; it does not support replacing surgical practice with hospital IT.

ontology_before: The aseptic-surgery edge was an unsourced generic enabling claim.
ontology_after: Aseptic surgery remains enabling clinical context for laparoscopic operations and is separated from hospital information infrastructure.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: aseptic surgery remains an enabling edge.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to simulator-only laparoscopic training outside patient operations.
A source showed laparoscopic surgery is not an operative clinical procedure.
The edge were upgraded to required in a way that over-modeled sterile practice as a device component.
short_reason: Laparoscopy is surgery, so aseptic surgical practice remains relevant context.
